metadata.summary: Escalate Roslyn analyzer severities to error and ship PublicApiAnalyzers to prevent LLM-introduced null derefs, async-void, and accidental public API breaks in .NET.

content.title: C# Roslyn Analyzers as Compile Errors + Public API Lock
content.summary: For every recurring class of LLM-introduced bug in a .NET codebase (null deref, async-void, missing ConfigureAwait, accidental public-API break), enable the corresponding Roslyn analyzer with severity error in .editorconfig and ship Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.PublicApiAnalyzers against checked-in PublicAPI.Shipped.txt + PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt files. The build refuses to compile until each failure mode is fixed, so Copilot/Claude-Code edits cannot land code that violates a known-bad pattern.

why.p: Pure-LLM review hallucinates concerns; pure regex linters miss intent. Roslyn analyzers run inside the compiler with full type and flow information, so they reject exactly the syntactic shapes that produced past production incidents — not vague "best practices". When severity is error, the agent's tool-use loop sees a build failure, reads the diagnostic, and self-corrects; when it is warning, the agent learns to ignore it. PublicApiAnalyzers additionally pin the library's public surface: any agent-added public type or method that is not in PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt is a build error, preventing accidental SemVer-major breakage. Together they convert the most common LLM regressions in C# from "spotted in review" to "never typed".

list.item: Production .NET libraries (NuGet packages, internal SDKs) where a SemVer-major break is expensive.
.NET services with Copilot/Claude/Codex authoring code regularly.
Codebases that have suffered repeated incidents from a single bug class (null deref, async-void, blocking .Result).
Multi-target frameworks (net8.0;net9.0) where consistency under all TFMs matters.
Throwaway scripts, scratch console apps, sample/demo projects — bootstrap cost dominates.
Internal apps with no public API surface — skip PublicApiAnalyzers, keep the bug-class analyzers.
Greenfield prototypes still churning the API every day — pin the analyzer set after the first stable release.

section.name: analyzer-as-error
section.p: Roslyn analyzers ship rich, type-aware diagnostics (CA, IDE, VSTHRD, CS rules). Severity is configured per-rule in .editorconfig. When set to error, a violation breaks the build instead of generating a warning the agent will silently dismiss in its tool-use loop.

rule.statement: Project repos MUST commit a top-level .editorconfig that escalates at least these analyzer ids to error: CS8602/CS8618/CS8625 (nullable reference warnings), CA2007 (missing ConfigureAwait), VSTHRD100 (async-void), VSTHRD002 (synchronous wait on async), CA2016 (missing CancellationToken). MSBuild MUST set TreatWarningsAsErrors=true at minimum for Release. Agents are forbidden from suppressing a diagnostic via #pragma warning disable or [SuppressMessage] without a same-line justification comment.
rule.rationale: An analyzer at warning severity is invisible to an LLM agent unless the harness surfaces it; at error it becomes a compile failure the agent must address before proceeding (per Microsoft .NET team's "make the bug class structurally impossible" pattern).

example.code:
# .editorconfig (root) root = true
[*.cs] dotnet_diagnostic.CS8602.severity = error # null deref dotnet_diagnostic.CS8618.severity = error # uninitialized non-null field dotnet_diagnostic.CA2007.severity = error # missing ConfigureAwait dotnet_diagnostic.VSTHRD100.severity = error # async void dotnet_diagnostic.VSTHRD002.severity = error # .Result / .Wait() dotnet_diagnostic.CA2016.severity = error # forward CancellationToken
# only the rule-specific suppression below is allowed [*Tests.cs] dotnet_diagnostic.CA2007.severity = none # tests run in known sync context
example.note: Bug classes are errors globally; suppression is scoped, narrow, and explained.
Suppression without comment hides the real fix (.ConfigureAwait(false)); the lock is bypassed.

antipattern.description: Setting TreatWarningsAsErrors=false in CI to "unblock" a flaky build instead of repairing the diagnostics.
antipattern.reason: Once warnings are not errors, every analyzer effectively becomes optional, and the next agent edit that triggers one slips through review.

section.reference: Severity escalation file for the canonical LLM bug classes.

section.name: public-api-lock
section.p: The Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.PublicApiAnalyzers NuGet ships RS0016 (declared symbol not in shipped/unshipped) and RS0017 (removed shipped API). Each project keeps two text files listing every public symbol; the analyzer emits an error on any drift.

rule.statement: Every published library project (csproj producing a NuGet package or referenced across team boundaries) MUST add PackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.PublicApiAnalyzers" and commit non-empty PublicAPI.Shipped.txt + PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt next to the csproj. RS0016 and RS0017 MUST be error severity. Adding a public symbol requires editing PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt in the same PR; removing one is a SemVer-major decision that requires a human approver label.
rule.rationale: An LLM agent extending a method's signature (adding a parameter, widening a return type) silently breaks every downstream consumer; PublicApiAnalyzers reduces that to a compile failure with the exact symbol, so the agent must either fix the signature or escalate.

example.note: Files declared as AdditionalFiles; analyzer reads them at compile time.
Analyzer catches the new public surface area; agent must update PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt.

antipattern.description: "Fix" RS0017 (removed API) by deleting the corresponding line from PublicAPI.Shipped.txt.
antipattern.reason: Shipped means already released to consumers; removing the line hides the breaking change instead of recording it. The lift goes through *REMOVED* markers in PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt with explicit human review.

section.reference: MSBuild props enabling the analyzer for every csproj.

template.purpose: Severity escalation snippet for the canonical LLM bug classes.
MSBuild props enabling analyzers + public-API analyzer for every project.
